title: Export data in other formats
excerpt: At our company, we use Procore. Currently, our project managers are uploading their MS Project schedules into Procore by exporting in the following formats (MPP, MPX, XER, PP, XML, PPX, FTS, POD, GAN, PEP, PRX, STX, CDPX…

posts.author: MrAwesome (Benjamin Walsh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Exporting to Excel™ is a great feature! Unfortunately, Excel™ formats dates and some other data types as numbers (11/3/2022 => 44868) when we share our information with other vendors.

We overcome this issue by opening in Excel™ and then exporting again as a comma separated values file. It’s not a huge inconvenience, but it would be wonderful if we could export boards and groups as plain text/.csv files.

posts.author: MASO (Markus @ Gorilla Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @MrAwesome

you might want to try Exported Excel Cleaner and Spreadsheet Gorilla, since both apps are capable to convert respectively export boards as .CSV files.

Let me know if you have any questions.

– Markus from Gorilla Apps

posts.author: mweekend (Matthew)
posts.cookedHtml: At our company, we use Procore. Currently, our project managers are uploading their MS Project schedules into Procore by exporting in the following formats (MPP, MPX, XER, PP, XML, PPX, FTS, POD, GAN, PEP, PRX, STX, CDPX, CDPZ, SP). I’m told this isn’t possible with Monday.com. However, adding this feature would help us move away from MS Project.
